pattern: Counting non-decreasing-digit numbers up to a bound is a classic digit dynamic program over the base-b representation, and the inclusive range is the difference of two prefix counts.
hints: Define g(x) = how many integers in [0, x] have non-decreasing base-b digits. The answer for [l, r] is g(r) - g(l-1).

Build x in base b digit by digit from most significant. Track the last digit placed, whether you are still bound by the prefix of x (tight), and whether the number has started (to skip leading zeros).
At each position you may place any digit from 0 up to the tight limit that is at least the previous placed digit; memoize on (position, last digit, tight, started) and take everything modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: single value range that is non-decreasing
lower bound 1 so 0 must be excluded by l-1

stress.description: random decimal range and base; large enough digit counts to exercise tight transitions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    b = rng.randint(2, 10)
    digits_lo = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(20, size)))
    lo = rng.randint(10**(digits_lo-1), 10**digits_lo - 1) if digits_lo > 1 else rng.randint(1, 9)
    span = rng.randint(0, 5000)
    hi = lo + span
    return [str(lo), str(hi), b]
stress.note: ranges can be large; the digit DP handles them while the oracle stays bounded by the chosen span
